title: Gramática Esencial en Diferentes Idiomas

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
2.1 English - Be verb
There are differtent kinds of words in a language.
nouns: A noun is a person, place or thing e.g. mother, teacher, baby, city, classroom, pen chair. There are diferent types of nouns
verbs: A verb is an action word e.g. cook, drink, laugh, dance
adjectives: An adjective is a word that describes a noun e.g. tall, hot, pink.
Be connects the subject to information about the subject. It isn’t an action word.
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
Subject (Person)
Form of to be (+ / -)
Example




I
am / am not
I am happy. (Estoy feliz.)


you (informal/formal, singular)
are / are not (aren’t)
You are nice. (Eres amable.)


he
is / is not (isn’t)
He is small. (Él es pequeño.)


she
is / is not (isn’t)
She is tired. (Ella está cansada.)


it
is / is not (isn’t)
It is cold. (Hace frío.)


we
are / are not (aren’t)
We are here. (Estamos aquí.)


you (plural)
are / are not (aren’t)
You are late. (Ustedes están retrasados.)


they
are / are not (aren’t)
They are loud. (Ellos son ruidosos.)




This is Jenifer
She is five years old
She has a brother
He is seven years old
What’s your name? –> My name is Lucas
I’m German. I’m from Berlin
I’m a student
My father is a Doctor and my mother is an engineer
My favourite color is blue
I’m interested in music
I’m not tired
He isn’t here
We aren’t students
I’m tired but I’m not hungry
The weather is nice today
Lucas is not at home, he is at work
Ana and I are good friends
It’s ten o’clock. Your’e late again
Pablo is not a teacher. He is a lawyer
My hands are cold


2.1.1 Practice conversations
Conversation1: Meeting someone new
A: Good day! I am Ms. Keller. What is your name? B: Good day, Ms. Keller. I am Mr. Fischer. A: Are you from Germany? B: No, I am not from Germany. I am from Austria. And you? A: I am from Berlin. B: Nice to meet you!
Conversation 2 — Talking to a friend
A: Hello! How are you? Are you tired? B: Yes, I’m a little tired. And you? A: No, I’m not tired; I’m hungry! B: We’re both at the café. The food here is good. A: Yes, you’re right!
Conversation 3 — Describing family
A: Is that your brother? B: Yes, that’s my brother. He is seventeen years old. A: And who is that? B: That’s my sister. She is a university student. A: Are you from Munich? B: No, we are from Hamburg.
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
2.2 German - SEIN
EINHEIT 1: bin / bist / ist / sind / seid / sind (Präsens von sein)








Subject (Person)
Form of sein (+ / −)
Example




ich (I)
bin / bin nicht
Ich bin glücklich. (I am happy.)


du (you – informal, singular)
bist / bist nicht
Du bist nett. (You are nice.)


er / sie / es (he / she / it)
ist / ist nicht
Er ist klein. (He is small.)


wir (we)
sind / sind nicht
Wir sind hier. (We are here.)


ihr (you – informal, plural)
seid / seid nicht
Ihr seid spät. (You are late.)


sie (they)
sind / sind nicht
Sie sind laut. (They are loud.)


Sie (you – formal, singular/plural)
sind / sind nicht
Sie sind willkommen. (You are welcome.)




Das ist Jennifer. (This is Jennifer.)
Sie ist fünf Jahre alt. (She is five years old.)
Sie hat einen Bruder. (She has a brother.)
Er ist sieben Jahre alt. (He is seven years old.)
Wie heißt du? – Ich heiße Lucas. (What’s your name? – My name is Lucas.)
Ich bin Deutscher. Ich komme aus Berlin. (I’m German. I’m from Berlin.)
> Note: If the speaker is female: Ich bin Deutsche.
Ich bin Student. (I’m a student.)
> Note: If the speaker is female: Ich bin Studentin.
Mein Vater ist Arzt und meine Mutter ist Ingenieurin. (My father is a doctor and my mother is an engineer.)
Meine Lieblingsfarbe ist Blau. (My favourite color is blue.)
Ich bin interessiert an Musik (I’m interested in music.)
Ich bin nicht müde. (I’m not tired.)
Er ist nicht hier. (He isn’t here.)
Wir sind keine Studenten. (We aren’t students.)
Ich bin müde, aber ich habe keinen Hunger. (I’m tired, but I’m not hungry.)
Das Wetter ist heute schön. (The weather is nice today.)
Lucas ist nicht zu Hause. Er ist bei der Arbeit. (Lucas is not at home. He is at work.)
Ana und ich sind gute Freunde. (Ana and I are good friends.)
Es ist zehn Uhr. Du bist schon wieder zu spät. (It’s ten o’clock. You’re late again.)
Pablo ist kein Lehrer. Er ist Anwalt. (Pablo is not a teacher. He is a lawyer.)
Meine Hände sind kalt. (My hands are cold.)
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python

title: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python
text:
3.1.1 Reading and Writing Files with Open
File handling is an essential aspect of programming, and Python provides built-in functions to interact with files. This guide will explore how to use Python’s open function to read the text files (‘.txt’ files).
There are two methods for opening the file using the file handling concept.
crumbs: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python

title: 4  Capítulo: SQL fundamentals
text:
4.1 Introduction to Databases and SQL
Welcome to the “SQL Fundamentals” course! In today’s world, data is a critical business asset. But, if we can’t tap into that data, if we can’t access and manipulate it, it’s wasted. SQL solves that problem. It’s a language of databases. It allows you to communicate with a database and get what you need. It’s a critical skill in every analyst or data scientist’s toolbox, and it appears on most job postings for those positions. You’re going to develop that skill within this course. By the end, you will learn to construct a wide variety of SQL statements. By the end, you will learn to construct a wide variety of SQL statements. We start with the basics, and then move to more advanced concepts – like joins, subqueries, common table expressions, and window functions. Finally, you’ll also learn how to optimize your SQL statements so they’re efficient even on extremely large data sets.
Review the Course Learning Outcomes
By the end of this course, you’ll be able to…

title: 6  Capítulo: Probability for Computer Science
text:
6.1 Introduction to Probability
The idea of supply chains is managing flows. And flows of what? Flows of product or material, flows of information, and flows of money.
Course Overview and Basic Tools
starts by providing a fundamental grounding in combinatorics, and then quickly moves into the basics of probability theory. We will then cover many essential concepts in probability theory, including particular probability distributions, properties of probabilities, and mathematical tools for analyzing probabilities. Finally, the last third of the class will focus on data analysis and machine learning as a means for seeing direct applications of probability in this exciting and quickly growing subfield of computer science.
crumbs: 6  Capítulo: Probability for Computer Science

title: 7  Data Visualization and Cloud Technologies
text:
In this course, we learn to use data visualization and cloud computing to support business analytics. In the cloud computing section, we use the cloud to build scalable data warehouses, analyze big data, and develop machine learning models. In the data visualization section, we create and share compelling visualizations that enhance decision-making.
In the first half of the course, you’ll learn how to tell compelling stories using data visualization. Then in the second half, we’ll learning about cloud computing. You’ll set up and manage a cloud data warehouse, build and deploy a machine learning model in the cloud, and analyze big data in a cloud environment. These skills will prepare you to add value to your career right away.
crumbs: 7  Data Visualization and Cloud Technologies

title: 8  Capítulo: Data Science with R
text:
8.1 Data Science
Data science is a multidisciplinary field that uses scientific methods, processes, algorithms and systems to extract knowledge and insights from structured and unstructured data. It combines aspects of statistics, computer science, and domain expertise to analyze and interpret complex data sets. Data scientists use various tools and techniques to clean, process, and analyze data, often employing machine learning algorithms to make predictions or uncover patterns. The goal of data science is to turn data into actionable insights that can inform decision-making and drive business strategies. It is widely used in various industries, including finance, healthcare, marketing, and technology, to solve complex problems and improve outcomes.
crumbs: 8  Capítulo: Data Science with R
